leagueBriefing: It was a night of high drama in Mexico as Toluca solidified their second-place standing with a convincing home win, while Santos Laguna finally broke their winless drought with a last-gasp strike against ten-man Tijuana. For the Diablos Rojos, it was about maintaining pressure at the top, but for Santos, the late heroics in the border city felt like a massive weight finally being lifted off their shoulders.

games.headline: Paulinho seals the deal as Toluca keep pace with league leaders
games.brief: Toluca continued their impressive run toward the Liguilla with a professional 3-1 victory over Juárez at the Nemesio Díez. After a cagey first half, Helinho's penalty broke the deadlock and set the stage for a dominant second period. While Juárez briefly threatened to spoil the party with a Guilherme Castilho strike, a late insurance goal from Paulinho deep in stoppage time ensured the three points stayed in Toluca, keeping them firmly in the hunt for the top seed.
bullets: Helinho converted a penalty in the 54th minute to open the floodgates for the hosts.
Antonio Briseño doubled the lead in the 70th minute with a well-placed finish.
Paulinho’s goal in the 98th minute marked his continued clinical form in front of goal this season.
Toluca dominated the shot count 18-9, reflecting their offensive control throughout the match.
Juárez remains outside the qualifying zone following their fourth loss in six matches.
games.context: The win keeps Toluca in second place, trailing the leaders by just two points, while Juárez's playoff hopes continue to fade.

highlights.title: Toluca vs Juárez | Liga MX - Jornada 10 Clausura 2026 | TUDN

games.headline: Ocejo strikes in the 100th minute to give Santos elusive first win
games.brief: Santos Laguna finally found their first win of the Clausura 2026 in the most dramatic way possible, scoring a winner in the 10th minute of stoppage time. Facing a Tijuana side that played with ten men for the final half-hour following Jackson Porozo's dismissal, Santos initially struggled to capitalize on their advantage. However, Jesús Ocejo's last-second heroics broke the hearts of the Xolos faithful and provided a season-saving result for the visitors.
bullets: Jesús Ocejo scored the match-winner in the 90+10' minute, the latest goal of the tournament so far.
Tijuana defender Jackson Porozo was sent off in the 59th minute, forcing the hosts into a defensive shell.
Ezequiel Bullaude scored his first goal of the campaign to give Santos the initial lead.
Alejandro Gómez had briefly equalized for the 10-man Xolos in the 72nd minute before the late heartbreak.
This victory marks Santos Laguna's first three-point haul after nine winless matches to start the year.
games.context: A potentially season-turning victory for Santos Laguna, while Tijuana misses a chance to leap into the top eight.
